title: Isaiah, Chapter 62
description: Isaiah chapter 62. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 66. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 66.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>δια ϲιων ου ϲιωπηϲωμαι · και δια ιελημ ουκ ανηϲω ˙ εωϲ αν εξελθη ωϲ φωϲ η δικαιοϲυνη μου · το δε ϲωτηριον μου · ωϲ λαμπαϲ καυθηϲεται · <sup>2</sup>και οψοντε εθνη την δικαιοϲυνην ϲου · και βαϲιλειϲ την δοξαν ϲου : και καλεϲι ϲε το ονομα ϲου το καινον . ὁ ο κϲ ονομαϲι αυτο : <sup>3</sup>και εϲη ϲτεφανοϲ καλλουϲ εν χιρι κυ · και διαδημα βαϲιλειαϲ εν χιρι θυ ϲου · <sup>4</sup>και ουκετι κληρονομθηϲη καταλελιμμενη · και η γη ϲου ου κληθηϲετε ερημοϲ ˙ ϲοι γαρ κληθηϲετε θελημα εμον · και τη γη ϲου οικουμενη · <sup>5</sup>και ωϲ ϲοι νοικων νεανιϲκοϲ παρθενω . ουτωϲ κατοικηϲουϲιν οι υϊοι ϲου μετα ϲου ˙ και εϲτε . ον τροπο ευφρανθηϲεται νυμφιοϲ επι νυμφην . ουτωϲ ευφραθηϲετε κϲ επι ϲοι ·
translation.text:
<sup>1</sup>For Zion’s sake will I not hold my peace, and for Jerusalem’s sake I will not rest, until the righteousness thereof go forth as brightness, and the salvation thereof as a lamp
that burneth. <sup>2</sup>And the Gentiles shall see thy righteousness, and all kings thy glory: and thou shalt be called by a new name, which the mouth of the LORD shall name. <sup>3</sup>Thou shalt also be a crown of glory in the hand of the LORD, and a royal diadem in the hand of thy God. <sup>4</sup>Thou shalt no more be termed Forsaken; neither shall thy land any more be termed Desolate: but thou shalt be called Hephzi-bah, and thy land Beulah: for the LORD delighteth in thee, and thy land shall be married. <sup>5</sup>For
as a young man marrieth a virgin,
so shall thy sons marry thee: and
as the bridegroom rejoiceth over the bride,
so shall thy God rejoice over thee.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>και επι των τιχεω ϲου ιλμ · κατεϲτηϲα φυλακαϲ ολην τη ημεραν · και ολην την νυκτα · ὁι δια τελουϲʼ ου ϲιωπηϲονται · μιμνηϲκομενοι κυ · <sup>7</sup>ου γαρ εϲτιν ϋμιν ο οικοϲ . εαν διορθωϲη : και ποιηϲη ϊλμ αγαυριαμα επι τηϲ γηϲ · <sup>8</sup>ωμοϲεν κϲ κατα τηϲ δοξηϲ αυτου · και κατα τηϲ δοξηϲ του βραχιονοϲ αυτου · ει ετι δωϲω τον ϲιτον ϲου τοιϲ εχθροιϲ ϲου · και ει ετι πιοτε υϊοι αλʼλοτριοι τον οινον ϲου · εφ ω εμοχθηϲαϲ · <sup>9</sup>αλλ οι ϲυναγοντεϲ · φαγοντε αυτα ˙ και πιονται ˙ εν τεϲ επαυλεϲιν ταιϲ αγιεϲ μου · <sup>10</sup>πορευεϲθε δια τω πυλων μου · και οδοποιηϲατε τω λαω μου · και τουϲ λιθουϲ τουϲ εκ τηϲ οδου διαριψατε · εξαρατε ϲυϲϲημον ειϲ τα εθνη ·
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>I have set watchmen upon thy walls, O Jerusalem,
which shall never hold their peace day nor night: ye that make mention of the LORD, keep not silence, <sup>7</sup>And give him no rest, till he establish, and till he make Jerusalem a praise in the earth. <sup>8</sup>The LORD hath sworn by his right hand, and by the arm of his strength, Surely I will no more give thy corn
to be meat for thine enemies; and the sons of the stranger shall not drink thy wine, for the which thou hast laboured: <sup>9</sup>But they that have gathered it shall eat it, and praise the LORD; and they that have brought it together shall drink it in the courts of my holiness. <sup>10</sup>Go through, go through the gates; prepare ye the way of the people; cast up, cast up the highways; gather out the stones; lift up a standard for the people.

translation.original_text: <sup>11</sup>μιϲθον · και το εργον αυτου προ προϲωπου αυτου · <sup>12</sup>και καλεϲι αυτο λαον αγιον · λελυτρωμενον απο κυ ˙ ϲυνεκληθη επιζητουμενη πολιϲ · και ου καταλελιμμενη ·
translation.text:
<sup>11</sup>Behold, the LORD hath proclaimed unto the end of the world, Say ye to the daughter of Zion, Behold, thy salvation cometh; behold, his reward
is with him, and his work before him. <sup>12</sup>And they shall call them, The holy people, The redeemed of the LORD: and thou shalt be called, Sought out, A city not forsaken.
